title: simplify logical selects
summary:
The original issue is a boolean-`select` canonicalization opportunity:  
`%a = select %x, %y, false` computes `%x && %y`, and `%r = select %a, true, %y` computes `(%x && %y) || %y`, which simplifies to just `%y` by absorption. So the motivating bug was that LLVM recognized the single nested-select form poorly, even though it is just `or(and(X,Y),Y)`.

The patch generalizes this in `simplifySelectInst` for safe boolean `select` forms when `TrueVal == 1` (logical OR shape: `select Cond, true, FalseVal`):  
- If `Cond` matches `logical_and(FalseVal, anything)` in either operand order, return `FalseVal`. This captures `(X && Y) || Y -> Y`.  
- If `FalseVal` matches `logical_and(Cond, anything)` in either operand order, return `Cond`. This captures `Y || (X && Y) -> Y`.  
Use of `m_c_LogicalAnd` is the key abstraction: it handles commuted operands and works over canonical boolean/logical forms rather than just one exact select nest.

`dev_tests` broaden coverage well beyond the motivating case:  
- both nesting/operand orders (`land_lor_left1/2`, `or_and_common_op_commute*`),  
- scalar and `<N x i1>` vector forms,  
- direct logical ops (`and`) and compare-produced booleans (`or_cmps`),  
- poison-sensitive lanes/constants (`*_poison`, `inseltpoison`),  
- degenerate identities like `select %x, false, %x -> false`,  
- and non-matching near-misses (`or_and_not_common_op`) to show the transform requires a shared operand.  

This is a good template: lift a single select-of-select case into a logical matcher, then test all commutations, type widths, and poison cases.
